skill.name: api-design-principles
skill.description: Design and review REST and GraphQL APIs using practical patterns for resources, schemas, pagination, errors, versioning, security, and documentation.
skill.summary: Guides REST and GraphQL API design with templates, checklists, and review criteria.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak-crypto and shell-backtick patterns, but review found those to be false positives caused by API terminology and Markdown code fences. No prompt injection, malware intent, secret exfiltration, or command execution was found. One medium issue remains: the FastAPI template calls itself production-ready while using wildcard host and CORS defaults that should be restricted before deployment.

medium_findings.title: Wildcard Host and CORS Defaults in API Template
medium_findings.description: The FastAPI template is described as production-ready but uses wildcard host validation and wildcard CORS origins. If copied into production without changes, this can permit unexpected host headers and cross-origin browser access. The comments reduce the risk because they tell users to configure specific production values.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wildcard values are directly present in executable FastAPI template code. Risk is moderated because the comments mark both settings as production TODOs and credentialed CORS is disabled.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography hits occur in API design prose, GraphQL examples, HTTP status guidance, or placeholder password hashing references. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic primitive being executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation or API terminology, not cryptographic code. A manual review found no executable weak crypto implementation in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Mistaken for Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown code fences and inline formatting around REST, GraphQL, and Python examples. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution, subprocess calls, shell command construction, or command injection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks delimit Markdown examples and are not parsed as Ruby or shell by the skill. A targeted search found no subprocess, os.system, exec, or eval usage.

low_findings.title: Example Network Literals Are Non-Exfiltrating
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL and IP findings are API documentation examples and a local FastAPI run command. No evidence found of outbound requests, credential transfer, tracking endpoints, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations show a server bind address and illustrative API URLs, not outbound network calls. The remaining risk is configuration quality, covered by the medium finding.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard Network Exposure Defaults
dangerous_patterns.description: The template defaults permit any trusted host and any CORS origin. These settings should be replaced with explicit production hosts and origins before deployment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is visible in executable template code. It is a configuration risk rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Design Reliable REST and GraphQL APIs
content.value_statement: API teams often struggle with inconsistent endpoints, unclear schemas, weak error models, and late design changes. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical guidance for planning, reviewing, and documenting REST and GraphQL APIs.
actual_capabilities: Explains resource-oriented REST endpoint design and HTTP method semantics.
Provides GraphQL schema patterns for types, inputs, payloads, pagination, directives, and errors.
Includes a FastAPI REST template with pagination, validation models, error responses, and example endpoints.
Supplies a pre-implementation checklist for security, documentation, testing, performance, and monitoring.
Compares REST and GraphQL choices for versioning, filtering, sorting, mutations, and pagination.
Helps review API specifications for consistency, usability, and maintainability.
limitations: Does not generate a complete production backend by itself.
Template code includes TODO settings that must be hardened before production use.
Does not validate live OpenAPI or GraphQL schemas automatically.
Does not replace security review for authentication, authorization, or data exposure.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Public API
use_cases.description: Use the REST and GraphQL guidance to choose resource names, schemas, pagination, errors, and versioning before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review an Existing API Contract
use_cases.description: Compare an API proposal against the checklist to find inconsistent methods, missing errors, weak pagination, or unclear documentation.

use_cases.title: Create Team API Standards
use_cases.description: Turn the included patterns into internal rules for endpoint naming, GraphQL mutation shape, deprecation, and response formats.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Endpoint List
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these REST endpoints for resource naming, HTTP methods, status codes, pagination, and error handling. Suggest concrete improvements: [paste endpoints].

prompt_templates.title: Design a CRUD API
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a REST API for [domain]. Include resources, endpoints, request and response shapes, pagination, filtering, status codes, and versioning guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a GraphQL Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this GraphQL schema for type design, nullable fields, mutations, pagination, authorization boundaries, and N+1 risks: [paste schema].

prompt_templates.title: Create API Governance Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Create API design standards for a team building REST and GraphQL services. Include naming, versioning, errors, security checks, documentation, and review criteria.

output_examples.input: Review a user management endpoint proposal.
output: Flags action-based endpoints and recommends plural resource collections.
Adds pagination and filtering rules for list operations.
Defines consistent 4xx and 5xx error response behavior.
Defines order, line item, customer, connection, and payload types.
Uses input and payload objects for mutations.
Calls out DataLoader and query complexity protections.

output_examples.input: Prepare an API checklist before implementation.
output: Groups checks by resource design, versioning, errors, security, testing, and monitoring.
Highlights decisions that must be documented before coding starts.

best_practices: Start with the checklist before writing controllers, resolvers, or generated clients.
Ask for concrete endpoint or schema reviews instead of broad API advice.
Replace template security TODOs with explicit production hosts, origins, and authentication rules.
anti_patterns: Using action-oriented REST paths when resource names and HTTP methods are clearer.
Copying the FastAPI template into production without changing wildcard network settings.
Removing GraphQL fields without a deprecation period or migration plan.

faq.question: Does this skill support both REST and GraphQL?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes REST endpoint guidance, GraphQL schema patterns, and comparison points for choosing each approach.

faq.question: Can it generate production-ready code?
faq.answer: It provides a useful FastAPI template, but teams must harden configuration, persistence, authentication, and deployment settings.

faq.question: Does it validate OpenAPI files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance and review criteria, but it does not run schema validation tools.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. Claude Code and Codex can use the references to review API designs and guide implementation changes.

faq.question: What security topics are covered?
faq.answer: The checklist covers input validation, authorization, CORS, HTTPS, secrets in responses, rate limits, and GraphQL complexity controls.

faq.question: What should I provide in a prompt?
faq.answer: Provide endpoints, schemas, requirements, error examples, or design goals so the assistant can give specific recommendations.
